skill.name: context-fundamentals
skill.description: Explains context engineering fundamentals for AI agents, including context windows, attention limits, progressive disclosure, budgeting, and context component design.
skill.summary: Learn how to design, budget, and debug context for AI agent systems.
skill.author: muratcankoylan

security_audit.summary: AI review found the static external-command findings to be false positives caused by Markdown code fences and explanatory examples, not executable shell calls. Most weak-cryptography findings are false positives from prose, but scripts/context_manager.py uses MD5 for duplicate-content detection and includes helper methods that read caller-supplied paths. The skill appears educational and non-malicious, but publication should warn users about local filesystem access if the helper script is used with untrusted input.

medium_findings.title: Unconstrained Local File Reads
medium_findings.description: ProgressiveDisclosureManager accepts summary_path and detail_path values and passes them directly to open(). The configured base_dir is not enforced, so a caller that controls these paths could request arbitrary readable local files when this helper is integrated into an agent workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file reads are direct and use caller-provided paths. The risk depends on integration context, so this is a medium-risk issue rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: MD5 Used for Non-Security Duplicate Detection
low_findings.description: The script uses hashlib.md5 to create a short fingerprint for duplicate-content detection. This is not used for authentication, integrity verification, password storage, or security decisions, so the weak-cryptography static finding is a low-risk false positive in this context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding code shows MD5 is only used to identify repeated content snippets. No evidence found that the hash protects secrets or controls trust decisions.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged Markdown backtick fences and explanatory examples as Ruby or shell execution. Review found no executable external command calls in SKILL.md or references/context-components.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Markdown blocks and prose examples. No evidence found of runtime shell invocation or command construction in those files.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Review Found No Attack Text
low_findings.description: The skill discusses system prompts and includes benign prompt-structure examples. No evidence found of instructions that tell the evaluator to skip analysis, override system instructions, or change the risk rating.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files contain educational material about prompt organization, not evaluator-targeted override language. The conclusion is based on review of all three scanned files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct open() on Caller-Controlled Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper reads summary_path and detail_path without path normalization, allowlisting, or base directory containment checks. This pattern can expose sensitive local files if path values come from user-controlled references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code path is explicit and the inputs originate from a reference dictionary. Exploitability depends on how consumers pass reference values.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Hash Algorithm in Utility Code
dangerous_patterns.description: hashlib.md5 is present in the helper script. The current use is non-security duplicate detection, but stronger hashes avoid confusion if the utility is later reused for trust decisions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: MD5 use is confirmed, but the surrounding logic does not show security-sensitive use. The concern is future misuse rather than current malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: AI agents often fail when too much, too little, or poorly structured context is provided. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical concepts for designing context windows, budgets, and progressive disclosure.
actual_capabilities: Explains the main components of AI agent context, including system prompts, tools, documents, history, and tool outputs.
Describes context windows, attention limits, and why larger context is not always better.
Provides guidance for progressive disclosure and just-in-time document loading.
Shows how to reason about context budgets, truncation, summaries, and attention-favored positions.
Includes a Python helper script for estimating tokens, building context sections, and loading references.
Helps diagnose context-related agent behavior and architecture tradeoffs.
limitations: Token counting is approximate and should not replace model-specific tokenizers in production.
The included Python helper is demonstration code and needs hardening before use with untrusted inputs.
The skill teaches concepts but does not automatically inspect or optimize a live agent system.
It does not provide vendor-specific limits for every current model.

use_cases.title: Design an Agent Context Strategy
use_cases.description: Plan which instructions, tools, documents, and history should be loaded at each stage of an agent workflow.

use_cases.title: Debug Context-Related Failures
use_cases.description: Identify whether poor outputs are caused by missing instructions, noisy tool results, long histories, or weak document retrieval.
use_cases.target_user: Developers maintaining Claude, Codex, or Claude Code workflows

use_cases.title: Teach Context Engineering Basics
use_cases.description: Give teams a shared vocabulary for context windows, progressive disclosure, token budgets, and context degradation.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Context Basics
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain context engineering fundamentals for an AI agent. Cover system prompts, tool definitions, documents, message history, and tool outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Review My Context Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this agent context design and identify missing, redundant, or poorly placed context. Suggest a better loading strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Context Budget
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a context budget for this agent workflow. Allocate tokens across instructions, tools, retrieved documents, history, and tool outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Design Progressive Disclosure
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a progressive disclosure strategy for this multi-step agent. Specify what should be preloaded, loaded on demand, summarized, or discarded.

output_examples.input: Explain why my agent forgets instructions during long tasks.
output_examples.output: A structured explanation covering attention dilution, message history growth, noisy tool outputs, and practical fixes such as summaries and high-priority instruction placement.

output_examples.input: Review an agent architecture that loads all documentation at startup.
output: Risk: startup context is too large and noisy.
Recommendation: load summaries first, then retrieve detailed files only when needed.
Follow-up: define triggers for loading, summarizing, and discarding context.

output_examples.input: Plan context for a coding assistant with repository access.
output_examples.output: A context allocation plan that separates system rules, project instructions, file references, recent messages, and command outputs.

best_practices: Load the smallest high-signal context that supports the current decision.
Keep critical instructions near attention-favored positions and repeat only when necessary.
Use summaries, references, and retrieval to manage long-running workflows.
anti_patterns: Loading every available document before the task requires it.
Treating a larger context window as a substitute for context curation.
Letting verbose tool outputs accumulate without summaries or pruning.

faq.question: What does this skill teach?
faq.answer: It teaches how AI agent context is structured, constrained, loaded, and optimized.

faq.question: Does it run commands or call external services?
faq.answer: No external command execution or network calls were found in the reviewed files.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The Python helper reads local files from supplied paths, which needs containment checks before use with untrusted input.

faq.question: Can it estimate token usage?
faq.answer: Yes, the helper script provides rough character-based token estimates for planning.

faq.question: Is it specific to one AI model?
faq.answer: No. The concepts apply broadly to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other agent systems.

faq.question: Should the helper script be used in production?
faq.answer: Only after adding model-specific tokenization and safe path handling for local file reads.
